s2.ptxt: Temporarily install the bolt to the vane pump assembly.
Install the vane pump assembly.
Using SST, install bolt (B) and tighten the 2 bolts.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 300 mm (11.81 in.).
This torque value is accurate when SST is parallel to the torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: CONNECT POWER STEERING FLUID PRESSURE SWITCH CONNECTOR

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector to the power steering fluid pressure switch.
Install a new gasket to the pressure feed tube assembly.
Temporarily connect the pressure feed tube assembly to the vane pump assembly with the union bolt.
Install the pressure feed tube assembly clamp with the bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Install the pressure feed tube assembly clamp in the correct position.

s2.ptxt: Fully tighten the union bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the stopper of the pressure feed tube assembly contacts the vane pump assembly securely as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: CONNECT NO. 1 FLUID RESERVOIR TO PUMP HOSE

s2.ptxt: Connect the No. 1 fluid reservoir to pump hose to the vane pump assembly with the clip.

item.ptxt: Connect the No. 1 fluid reservoir to pump hose with the paint mark facing toward the rear of the vehicle.
Push the No. 1 fluid reservoir to pump hose as far as it will go as shown in the illustration.
Install the clip at the position specified in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FAN AND GENERATOR V BELT

s2.ptxt: Install the V-ribbed belt.
Using SST, turn the belt tensioner counterclockwise and remove the bar.
If it is difficult to install the V-ribbed belt, perform the following procedure:

s3.ptxt: Put the V-ribbed belt on every pulley except the tensioner pulley as shown in the illustration.
While releasing the belt tension by turning the belt tensioner counterclockwise, put the V-ribbed belt on the tensioner pulley.

item.ptxt: Put the backside of the V-ribbed belt on the tensioner pulley and idler pulley.
Check that the V-ribbed belt is properly set to each pulley.

s3.ptxt: After installing the V-ribbed belt, check that it fits properly in the ribbed grooves. Confirm that the belt has not slipped out of the grooves on the bottom of the crank pulley by hand.

s2.ptxt: Check the fluid level (See page ).
Jack up the front of the vehicle and support it with stands.
Turn the steering wheel.

s3.ptxt: With the engine stopped, turn the steering wheel slowly from lock to lock several times.

s2.ptxt: Lower the vehicle.
Start the engine.

s3.ptxt: Run the engine at idle for a few minutes.

s2.ptxt: Turn the steering wheel.

s3.ptxt: With the engine idling, turn the steering wheel left or right to the full lock position and keep it in that position for 2 to 3 seconds, then turn the steering wheel to the opposite full lock position and keep it there for 2 to 3 seconds.

s2.ptxt: Stop the engine.
Check for foaming or emulsification.
If the system has to be bled twice because of forming or emulsification, be sure to check for fluid leaks in the system.
Check the fluid level (See page ).
Keep the vehicle level.
With the engine stopped, check the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
If necessary, add fluid.

specitem.ptxt: ATF DEXRON II or III, or equivalent

atten4.ptxt: If the fluid is hot, check that the fluid level is within the HOT range on the fluid reservoir. If the fluid is cold, check that the fluid level is within the COLD range.

s2.ptxt: Start the engine and run it at idle.
Turn the steering wheel from lock to lock several times to raise fluid temperature.
Check for foaming or emulsification.
If foaming or emulsification is identified, bleed the power steering system (See page ).
With the engine idling, measure the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
Stop the engine.
Wait a few minutes and remeasure the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
If a problem is found, bleed the power steering system (See page ).
Check the fluid level.

s-1.ptxt: CHECK FOR POWER STEERING FLUID LEAKS
INSTALL FRONT FENDER APRON SEAL RH
